svg.text: Fig.1 Before — chat lifecycle today (one inbound message)
INBOUND → IDENTITY → DISPATCH → PROVIDER → EVENT LOG → HEARTBEAT → MEMORY → INDEX
· "Athena forgot what we said in Discord"
· Two TEAM column entries for same persona
· Quest claimed in UI invisible from CLI
· Cost meter split across N rows
· Skills trust set on one row, missing on the other
· Provider JSONL (Claude / Codex / Gemini) — provider-owned. Hammurabi reads via resume flag, never writes.
· Hammurabi event log — 2 copies per stream session: agents/sessions/<name>/transcript.v1.jsonl + commander/<id>/sessions/<tid>.jsonl
· Memory + LanceDB index — commander-level (one MEMORY.md, one working-memory, one index per id) — but FORKED via channel triple.
· Channel binding (channelMeta) lives on CommanderSession itself — single-valued — which is why two channels = two identities.
